title: Hip arthroplasty system and method
abstract: Apparatuses, systems and methods related to hip arthroplasty are disclosed. According to one example, the apparatus can include a table attachment and a positioning apparatus. The table attachment can have a plate with a first major surface and can be attachable to and detachable from an operating table. The positioning apparatus can be configured to hold at least a distal portion of a leg of a patient. The positioning apparatus can be attachable to the first major surface in a desired location for positioning the leg of the patient as desired. The positioning apparatus can have a first portion that is selectively positionally adjustable to allow the leg to be moveable in three degrees of freedom while the positioning apparatus holds the least the distal portion of the leg and is attached to the table attachment in the desired location.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. An assembly for use in a hip arthroplasty comprising: a table attachment including a plate and having a first major surface, wherein the table attachment is attachable to and detachable from an operating table; and a positioning apparatus configured to hold at least a distal portion of a leg of a patient during the hip arthroplasty, wherein the positioning apparatus is moveable relative to the first major surface to a desired location on the plate and is attachable to the first major surface in the desired location for positioning the leg of the patient as desired, wherein the positioning apparatus has a first portion that is selectively positionally adjustable to allow the leg to be moveable in three degrees of freedom while the positioning apparatus holds the least the distal portion of the leg and is attached to the table attachment in the desired location. 2. The assembly of claim 1, wherein the distal portion of the leg includes at least a foot of the patient. 3. The assembly of any one or any combination of claims 1-2, wherein the first portion of the positioning apparatus comprises a boot assembly that holds at least the foot, and wherein the assembly further includes: a base configured to rest on the first major surface and attach thereon; and a ball and socket joint coupling the base to the boot assembly. 4. The assembly of claim 3, wherein a portion of the boot assembly is selectively detachable from and attachable to the ball and socket joint. 5. The assembly of any one or any combination of claims 3-4, wherein the base attaches to the first major surface via a suction cup. 6. The assembly of claim 5, wherein the base includes an actuator configured to pivot to facilitate a vacuum within the suction cup. 7. The assembly of any one or any combination of claims 3-6, wherein the ball and socket joint is selectively positionally lockable to fix a position of the boot assembly and is selectively positionally unlockable to allow for rotational adjustment of the boot assembly to allow for movement of the leg in the three degrees of freedom. 8. The assembly of any one or any combination of claims 1-7, wherein the three degrees of freedom comprise rotational degrees of freedom and the leg of the patient is rotatable in roll, pitch and yaw. 9. A system for use in a hip arthroplasty comprising: a table attachment including a plate and having a first major surface, wherein the table attachment is configured to attach to and detach from an operating table; and a positioning apparatus configured to hold at least a foot of a patient during the hip arthroplasty, wherein the positioning apparatus is configured to be moveable relative to the first major surface to a desired location on the plate and is configured to attach to the first major surface in the desired location for positioning a leg of the patient as desired, wherein the positioning apparatus has a boot assembly that holds at least the foot of the patient and is selectively rotationally positionally adjustable while the positioning apparatus holds the least the distal portion of the leg and is attached to the table attachment in the desired location. 10. The system of claim 9, further comprising: a base configured to rest on the first major surface and attach thereon; and a ball and socket joint configured to couple the base to the boot assembly. 11. The system of claim 10, wherein a portion of the boot assembly is selectively detachable from and attachable to the ball and socket joint. 12. The system of any one or any combination of claims 10-11, wherein the base is configured to attach to the first major surface via a suction cup. 13. The system of claim 12, wherein the base includes an actuator configured to pivot to facilitate a vacuum within the suction cup. 14. The system of any one or any combination of claims 10-13, wherein the ball and socket joint is selectively positionally lockable to fix a position of the boot assembly and is selectively positionally unlockable to allow for rotational adjustment of the boot assembly to move the leg while the positioning apparatus is attached to the table attachment in the desired location. 15. A hip arthroplasty method comprising: attaching a table attachment to an operating table; receiving a distal portion of a leg of the patient within a positioning apparatus; positioning the patient on the operating table such that the positioning apparatus and the leg of the patient are supported by a table attachment; positioning the leg by moving the positioning apparatus relative to the table attachment; affixing the positioning apparatus to the table attachment when a desired location of the leg is achieved; and adjusting a rotational position of the leg while the positioning apparatus retains the distal portion of the leg and is affixed to the table attachment in the desired location. 16. The method of claim 15, wherein affixing includes generating a vacuum in a suction cup attached to the positioning apparatus against the table attachment. 17. The method of any one or any combination of claims 15-16, wherein adjusting the rotational position of the leg includes selectively positionally locking the positioning apparatus to fix a position of a rotatable portion thereof and 

 selectively positionally unlocking the positioning apparatus to allow for rotational adjustment of the rotatable portion to move the leg while the positioning apparatus is attached to the table attachment in the desired location. 18. The method of any one or any combination of claims 15-17, further comprising detaching a second portion of the positioning apparatus from a remainder of the positioning apparatus to check one or both of a length of the leg and an impingement of a hip joint. 19. The method of claim 18, further comprising reattaching the second portion of the positioning apparatus to the remainder of the positioning apparatus to during a check of the stability of the hip joint.
